## [09:04 - 12:03]

Maybe I've actually seen people Succeed in hitting the ball and it bounces backwards Amazing enough it can happen or perfectly sideways so they're Further away from the hole than they were before that shot and then they walk up and they take another swing yet and maybe this time it dribbles 30 feet in the general direction of the hole But what they do is they gradually course correct Until they get so they're only a couple inches away and they can tap the ball in the hole and even absolute beginners Like I said 12 maybe 13 or 14 shots and they can cover that third of a mile and get that ball in a hole Because you course correct you hit the ball in the generally in the correct direction And even though it's not perfect you course correct You get closer and closer and as you get closer to the hole the course corrections are smaller and smaller and that's the way there's a business You take an action and then you assess and then you take another action and you assess and you gradually move in the right direction, but Unless you take that action you can't course correct You can't and that's where we come back to the successful people having a huge bias towards action Now one of the things you need to do and this is big for them for your inner game for your mental game is Protect your confidence There's going to be people that are dream stealers You have to keep them out of your life Some of them they'll do it with good intentions because they think you're They they they love you and they care about you and they don't want you to see you get burned They don't want to see you waste your time.
